title: Phishing Campaign Targeting Local Bank Customers
description: Multiple residents have reported receiving fake emails impersonating First National Bank asking for account verification. The emails contain malicious links that redirect to a spoofed login page. Do not click any links. Contact your bank directly via their official website.

title: Downed Power Line on Elm Street
description: A power line has fallen across Elm Street near the intersection with Oak Avenue following last night's storm. The area is cordoned off by city crews. Avoid the block between 4th and 5th. Estimated repair time is 6 hours.

title: Flash Flood Warning Issued for Downtown Area
description: The National Weather Service has issued a flash flood warning valid until 8 PM tonight. Residents in low-lying areas near Riverside Drive should move valuables to upper floors and avoid driving through flooded roadways. Emergency shelters are open at City Hall and Lincoln High School.

title: Grandparent Scam Wave Reported
description: Several elderly residents have reported receiving calls from someone claiming to be a grandchild in legal trouble needing emergency wire transfers. This is a known scam. Hang up and call your family member directly using a number you already have. Do not send money or gift cards.

title: Local Healthcare Provider Data Breach
description: Westside Medical Group has disclosed a data breach affecting approximately 12,000 patient records including names, addresses, and insurance information. Affected patients are being notified by mail. Monitor your credit report and explanation of benefits statements for unauthorized activity.

title: Suspicious Package Near Community Center
description: Police responded to a report of a suspicious unattended package near the Northside Community Center on Park Boulevard. The bomb squad conducted an assessment and the package was determined to be harmless. The area has been cleared and the community center has reopened.

title: Gas Leak at Maple Street Apartments
description: A gas leak was detected at the Maple Street Apartment complex. Residents of buildings A and B have been evacuated. Do not re-enter until the all-clear is issued by the utility company. If you smell gas in the area, leave immediately and call 911.

title: Package Theft Spike in Residential Area
description: Residents in the Maplewood neighborhood have reported a significant increase in porch package theft over the past two weeks. Police are patrolling the area. Recommendations: require a signature for deliveries, use a package locker, or have packages delivered to your workplace.

title: Public Wi-Fi Credential Harvesting Attack
description: Security researchers have identified a rogue access point mimicking the free Wi-Fi network at Central Library. Connecting to it may expose your login credentials. Use only verified networks, enable VPN, and avoid accessing sensitive accounts on public Wi-Fi until this is resolved.

title: Extreme Heat Advisory Through the Weekend
description: Temperatures are expected to reach 108°F this weekend. Cooling centers are open at all public libraries and the Convention Center. Check on elderly neighbors and pets. Avoid outdoor activity between 10 AM and 6 PM. Stay hydrated and watch for signs of heat exhaustion.

title: Fake Tech Support Calls Targeting Seniors
description: Residents are receiving calls from people claiming to be Microsoft or Apple support, warning about viruses on their computer and requesting remote access. This is a scam. Legitimate tech companies do not make unsolicited calls. Hang up immediately and do not grant remote access.

title: Bridge Structural Inspection Causes Traffic Delays
description: The Harrison Street Bridge is undergoing emergency structural inspection following reports of cracking. One lane remains open. Expect significant delays. Consider using the Market Street or Congress Avenue bridges as alternates. Inspection expected to conclude by end of week.

title: Ransomware Attack on City Library System
description: The city library system has been hit by a ransomware attack, taking all digital services offline including the catalog, eBook lending, and computer access. Physical libraries remain open. IT teams are working to restore services. No patron personal data is believed to have been exfiltrated.

title: Vehicle Break-Ins Near Riverside Park
description: Eight vehicle break-ins have been reported in the Riverside Park parking lot over the past week, primarily on weekday evenings. Police have increased patrols. Do not leave valuables visible in your car. Park in well-lit areas where possible.

title: Water Main Break Causes Outage in Westside District
description: A water main break on Jefferson Avenue has caused a service outage affecting approximately 400 homes and businesses in the Westside District. Crews are on site. Boil water advisory is in effect for affected areas once service is restored. Expected repair window: 4-8 hours.

title: Tornado Watch Issued for Northern Counties
description: A tornado watch has been issued for all northern counties until midnight. Conditions are favorable for tornado development. Identify your safe room now (interior room, lowest floor). Have a battery-powered weather radio ready. Monitor local alerts and be prepared to take shelter immediately.

title: QR Code Parking Meter Scam Downtown
description: Fraudulent QR code stickers have been placed over legitimate QR codes on downtown parking meters, directing drivers to a fake payment site that steals credit card information. Use the official ParkMobile app or pay at the meter directly. Report suspicious stickers to the Parking Authority.

title: School Zone Crosswalk Light Malfunction
description: The pedestrian crossing signal at Parkview Elementary on Cedar Street is malfunctioning and showing conflicting signals. The city has been notified. Until repairs are complete, a crossing guard has been stationed during school hours. Drivers: please slow down and yield to pedestrians.

title: SMS Smishing Campaign Targeting Chicago Residents
description: Chicago residents are receiving text messages impersonating the Illinois Tollway claiming an unpaid toll balance of $3.45. The link leads to a credential-harvesting site. Do not click the link. The Illinois Tollway does not request payment via SMS. Report the message to the FTC at reportfraud.ftc.gov.

title: Sinkhole Opens on South Michigan Avenue
description: A sinkhole approximately 8 feet wide has opened on South Michigan Avenue near the intersection with 18th Street following heavy rainfall. The block is closed to vehicle and pedestrian traffic. City engineers are on site assessing the damage. Expect significant traffic rerouting in the South Loop area. Use alternate routes.
